gap_summaries: [ultrathink][critical][fixed] test_running_node_refuses_re_entry asserted RunnerError but W0-M01 scheduler already filters non-PENDING nodes - the pre-condition guard was unreachable. Rewrote the test to assert result is None (the scheduler-filter behavior) and removed the dead defensive guard from run_next_node. Aligns with design decision Sec 5 ('runner does not enforce state-transition legality').

[ultrathink][critical][fixed] Task 10 left the FAILED-path emit sites and error_class mapping implicit, with 8 distinct FAILED branches across Tasks 7/8/9. High risk of the implementer missing an emit site and shipping a partially-correlated telemetry stream. Added an explicit (branch, reason, error_class) table in Task 10 enumerating all 8 FAILED paths plus the 1 Started and 2 Completed sites; total emit sites now spelled out (1 + 2 + 8).

[ultrathink][critical][fixed] NodeRun.attempts was never incremented; LifecyclePhaseFailed.attempt=status.nodes[node_id].attempts would always read 0. Added attempts += 1 in run_next_node immediately before the RUNNING transition (Task 7), so a failed node's emit carries attempt=1 and a future failure-governance milestone (Wave 8 Sec 38) can layer retry/escalation on the same counter without schema changes.

[ultrathink][important][fixed] Task 10 test bodies referenced list[Any] annotations without importing Any. Added 'from typing import Any' at the top of tests/test_lifecycle_runner.py in Task 6 so all subsequent telemetry tests resolve cleanly.

[ultrathink][important][fixed] monitor_rc semantics ambiguous - commands.tmux.cmd_monitor_session always returns rc=0 in normal flow (terminal state surfaces via JSON stdout, not return code), so the monitor_nonzero FAILED branch in Task 7 would never fire in production wiring. Added an inline comment documenting the contract: monitor_rc != 0 only fires on monitor_session itself raising or a custom monitor surfacing rc; 'session crashed but rc=0' is decided downstream by the verifier (artifact_exists returning verified=False -> verifier-rejected FAILED).

[ultrathink][important][fixed] Task 12 acceptance test fake_spawn computed a dead node_id variable from fragile string parsing that was never used - the for-loop below did the actual match. Removed the dead computation; kept the clean substring-match.

[ultrathink][important][fixed] Task 10 placement of LifecyclePhaseStarted emit was ambiguous re: the phase-4 branch inserted by Task 8. The Started event MUST fire for both delegate and spawn paths (Task 12 phase-4 acceptance asserts this). Reworded Task 10 Step 3 to make the ordering explicit: 'after RUNNING transition and attempts increment, after run_id read, BEFORE the phase-4 branch'.

[ultrathink][deferred] Coverage --source argument accepts both filesystem paths and dotted module names; W0-M01 used the filesystem-path form and landed cleanly, so kept the same form for W0-M02 to minimize CI drift. Recordkeeping only.

[ultrathink][deferred] _session_name_for_node uses a shape different from the existing core.tmux_runtime.generate_session_name (step/epic/story_id/cycle tuple vs node/run_id). W0-M04 production wiring will own the adapter that maps node fields to the existing tmux session-name convention; for W0-M02 the runner-internal name is sufficient because every test stubs the spawn boundary.

[ultrathink][deferred] last_error field stored verbatim from monitor / spawn / delegate output - no length cap. A pathological multi-megabyte stderr would bloat the status file. Non-issue for Sec 2 acceptance fixtures; revisit if observability tests exercise large outputs.
scope_notes: Phase A is plan-only - no production code or tests committed in this phase, so 'tests_green: true' reflects that the existing W0-M01 suite remains untouched and 'lint_clean: true' reflects that no source files were modified.
Plan file: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-17-w0-m02-phase-runner-verifiers.md (14 tasks, TDD-structured, mirrors W0-M01 task-card style).

Acceptance criteria from build-spec-full.md Sec 2 (Wave 0) all mapped to specific tasks: spawn+monitor -> Task 7; verifier registry -> Tasks 3-5; phase-4 delegate -> Task 8; new lifecycle_events.py -> Tasks 1-2; end-to-end acceptance mirrors test_orchestration_loop.py -> Task 12.

Hard guardrails enforced by construction: core/telemetry_events.py never edited (events.py imports Event, subclasses it); core/success_verifiers.py never edited (new lifecycle_verifiers.py is a sibling); no new third-party imports (audit step in Task 13).
